rulings.Case Title: ALBERT WRIGHT JR` et al VS. A.W. CHERSTON COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 21, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT GOULDS PUMPS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Failure to shift burden. See Weber vs. John Crane (2006) 143 Cal.App. 4th 1433; Gaggero vs. Yura (2003) 108 Cal.App.4th at 893. =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LORIN LEWIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Stay Or Dismiss Action On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 21, 2009, line 3, DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER LLC'S MOTION To Stay Or Dismiss Action On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens. Motion is denied. Insufficient showing that Montana is a suitable alternative forum. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: LORIN LEWIS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY BONHACH VS. MALIBU PIER PARTNERS LLC A DELAWARE PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 21, 2009, line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT JEFFREY BONHACH'S MOTION TO STRIKE THIRD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Motion to Strike is denied. No showing that the cross-complaint arises from protected activity. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY BONHACH VS. MALIBU PIER PARTNERS LLC A DELAWARE PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 21, 2009, line 5, CROSS DEFENDANT JEFFREY BONHACH'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Demurrer is overruled in part, and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to the first cause of action for Breach of Contract. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for Breach of Duty of Loyalty and third cause of action for Breach of Duty of Confidentiality. Failure to state a basis for assertions that the cross-defendant had a fiduciary responsibility to cross-complainant. No facts to support a confidential relationship. No reasonable probability that defects can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: MARK L WEBB VS. JULIA FOLLANSBEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 21, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT JULIA FOLLANSBEE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demuerrer is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to plead damage or an actionable misrepresentation. Generally an actional misrepresentation must be made as to past or existing facts. Predictions as to future events, or statements as to future action by some third party, are deemed opinions, and are not actionable fraud. Borba vs. Thomas (1977) 70 Cal.App. 3rd 144. =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MARK L WEBB VS. JULIA FOLLANSBEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 21, 2009, line 10, DEFENDANT JULIA FOLLANSBEE'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Motion to quash is denied. Defendant reached out to California to transact business. Plaintiff's claim directly arises from that contact. Motion to dismiss on grounds of forum nonconveniens is denied. Although Oregon is likely a suitable forum, insufficient evidence whether the balance of public and private interests shows that it would be more just to file the case elsewhere. Defendant fails to show that California is a seriously inconvenient forum. =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LORRAINE BRADDICK VS. KAREN FLETCHER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Lorraine Braddick'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 21, 2009, line 16, Plaintiff Lorraine Braddick's Motion for Summary Judgment (transferred from Department 301, per challenge to Judge Quidachay). Plaintiff's Motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff shifts the burden by establishing her prima facie unlawful detainer case and that defendant cannot establish any affirmative defenses. =(302/CWW/ER)

rulings.Case Title: LORRAINE BRADDICK VS. KAREN FLETCHER et al
